resultRaw.sql:
SELECT
  c.id AS course_id,
  c.code AS course_code,
  c.name AS course_name,
  COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count
FROM public.courses AS c
LEFT JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
HAVING COUNT(e.id) = 0
ORDER BY c.code ASC;

issues: All encoded fields (course_id, course_code, course_name, enrollment_count) exist in metadata.columns — OK.
Since enrollment_count = 0 for all rows, bar marks will have zero height and may be invisible; consider using text labels, a table, or another mark type to make zero-count courses visible.
SQL orders by course_code ASC but the x-axis is course_name; the visual display order may not reflect the SQL ORDER BY (potential mismatch between data ordering and axis encoding).
Color encoding by course_code is redundant with course_name on the x-axis and may add unnecessary legend clutter; it’s a presentation choice rather than a correctness issue.
